claim: Context chaining - prefixing prompts with 'update [file]: [function]:' - allows LLM code generators to precisely target specific code locations for changes
actionable: When using AI coding assistants for multi-file projects, specify the exact file and function to modify in your prompts
source_title: AI Coding Devlog - Aider ON Sonnet 3.5 - CURATE your ELITE information DIET

claim: Agentic workflow structure (Retrieve-Agentic-Act-Learn-Notify) creates repeatable patterns for AI-assisted work that compound over time
actionable: Structure AI agent tasks as: 1) Retrieve context/data, 2) Run AI reasoning/planning, 3) Take action, 4) Learn from outcomes, 5) Notify stakeholders
source_title: AI Coding Devlog - Aider ON Sonnet 3.5 - CURATE your ELITE information DIET

claim: Markdown formatting for prompts creates more readable and maintainable prompts than plain text, improving collaboration and debugging
actionable: Use markdown headers, bullet points, and code blocks to structure complex prompts for AI assistants
source_title: AI Coding Devlog - Aider ON Sonnet 3.5 - CURATE your ELITE information DIET

claim: Using JSON structured outputs with specific response schemas forces models to return data in predictable formats for downstream automation
actionable: Define expected JSON output structure in your prompts and ask model to validate its response matches that schema
source_title: AI Coding Devlog - Aider ON Sonnet 3.5 - CURATE your ELITE information DIET

claim: Information diet curation - focusing on high-signal sources (blogs, changelogs, podcasts from builders) rather than social media noise - improves decision quality
actionable: Subscribe to technical blogs and changelogs from the tools/technologies you use; check daily/weekly rather than following social media
source_title: AI Coding Devlog - Aider ON Sonnet 3.5 - CURATE your ELITE information DIET

claim: Streaming UI updates with st.rerun() in Streamlit requires coordination with agentic workflow steps to avoid race conditions
actionable: Call UI update methods after agent workflow functions complete, not in parallel, to ensure UI stays in sync with agent state
source_title: AI Coding Devlog - Aider ON Sonnet 3.5 - CURATE your ELITE information DIET
